content.text:
Now I have all the info. Let me analyze the asm:

- `r4 = gobj->user_data` (Item*)
- `f1 = 0.0f` (default)
- `r0 = ip->kind` (offset 0x10)
- `r3 = ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes` (itMatadogasAttributes*)
- Switch: if kind == 0xC2, f1 = attrs->x8; if kind == 0xC1, f1 = attrs->x4; else f1 stays 0.0f
- Scale velocity: `ip->x40_vel.x *= f1; ip->x40_vel.y *= f1; ip->x40_vel.z *= f1`

Wait — the branch logic with `bge` after `cmpwi r0, 0xC2`:
- `cmpwi r0, 0xC2; beq .L_802CB878` → 0xC2 goes to attrs->x8
- `bge .L_802CB87C` → > 0xC2 goes to default
- `cmpwi r0, 0xC1; bge .L_802CB870` → >= 0xC1 (which is just 0xC1 since 0xC2 handled) goes to attrs->x4
- `b .L_802CB87C` → else default

So it's a switch: 0xC1 → attrs->x4, 0xC2 → attrs->x8, else → 0.0f

But wait, `itMatadogasAttributes` only has x0, x4, x8, xC as float fields. And the attrs pointer is `ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`. So attrs->x4 and attrs->x8 are the second and third floats.

Let me also check what 0xC1 and 0xC2 mean in ItemKind. Looking at the existing code, `it_802CB4F0(gobj, 0xC1, attrs->x8)` passes 0xC1 as an argument that becomes a motion/kind value. Let me check if there are enum names.
